typecheck.command: xcodebuild build-for-testing -project OpenBurnBar.xcodeproj -scheme OpenBurnBar -destination "platform=macOS,arch=arm64" -clonedSourcePackagesDirPath .spm-cache -derivedDataPath .derived-data/ci-typecheck CODE_SIGNING_ALLOWED=NO CODE_SIGNING_REQUIRED=NO

blockingIssues.description: VAL-EXEC-001 failed/cancelled coverage still validates result status only; packet-level terminal status and `completedAt` mapping assertions are not present in `testVAL_EXEC_001_*` coverage.

syncMissionExecution introduces a regression: `if isTerminalPhase || desiredPacketStatus != packet.status || packet.completedAt == nil` is true for non-terminal packets, causing repeated `mission_packet_synced` rewrites and `updatedAt` churn even without state changes.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Replace outdated `OpenBurnBar*` XCTest filter examples with canonical `.factory/services.yaml` `BurnBar*` commands and require focused-command evidence capture to prevent command/evidence drift during reruns.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Both round-3 fix reviews independently flagged skill-command mismatch (`OpenBurnBar*` vs `BurnBar*`) and handoff drift away from focused verification commands, indicating a repeated systemic process issue.
